title: Living in Boulder vlog 2 - 4 - 2018: Google Earthing Circus Maximus stands

enrichment.summary: Kyle vlogs from Boulder late at night while working on a Google Earth 3D model of the Circus Maximus in ancient Rome. He describes his progress building the stadium stands, muses on the Circus Maximus's massive capacity compared to modern stadiums, and plans his next steps including modeling other Roman circuses and eventually the full city.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Initially dismissed it as just a race course, now thinks it's more amazing than the Colosseum because of its sheer size
Considers it the biggest legitimate stadium ever built, dwarfing even the largest modern stadiums
Doesn't consider modern race track venues legitimate stadiums in the same category as traditional stadiums
Finds them an amazing way to study history and recommends reading them in sequence

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: It's 10:21 PM on February 4th, and I'm not totally tired right now.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I just felt like doing a video now.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I think I did a video two nights ago because I got too tired and didn't do it last night.

claims.subject: Circus Maximus
claims.claim: Today is my second full day working on the Circus Maximus.

claims.subject: Circus Maximus
claims.claim: On the first day, I put bricks and started with the stands.

claims.subject: Circus Maximus
claims.claim: Today I was having trouble putting the lines of the stands because it wasn't staying in the same place due to its length and elevation changes.

claims.subject: Circus Maximus
claims.claim: I figured out how to fix the stands, but it's slow going.

claims.subject: Circus Maximus
claims.claim: If I keep working for another few hours, I could finish the stands tonight.

claims.subject: Circus Maximus
claims.claim: Once the stands are done, I can start designing it and figuring out how to connect the Palatine Hill to the Circus Maximus.

claims.subject: Circus Maximus
claims.claim: The Circus Maximus is a giant stadium, and the top of it is the same height as the Palatine Hill next to it.

claims.subject: Circus Maximus
claims.claim: I'm really looking forward to finding out exactly how big the Circus Maximus is.

claims.subject: Circus Maximus
claims.claim: The Wikipedia article says Pliny claimed it could hold 250,000 people, but I couldn't find that in Pliny's writings.

claims.subject: Circus Maximus
claims.claim: The region here at Calagra for four says it could hold 255,000 people, but I think that's a feat.

claims.subject: Colosseum
claims.claim: The Colosseum has 22 kilometers worth of stands, but I don't know how many Roman feet that is.

claims.subject: Colosseum
claims.claim: If the Colosseum has 87,000 stands, I want to find out how many Roman feet that is.

claims.subject: Circus Maximus
claims.claim: I'm not sure how many stands I'm going to have for the Circus Maximus yet.

claims.subject: Circus Maximus
claims.claim: When I was working on the Colosseum, I thought it was the most interesting thing ever, but now I think the Circus Maximus is the most amazing thing ever because it's so big.

claims.subject: Circus Maximus
claims.claim: The Wikipedia article says the Circus Maximus could hold 150,000 people, which is still the biggest stadium ever.

claims.subject: Modern stadiums
claims.claim: The biggest stadium in America is one of the football teams' stadiums, which holds 106,000 people.

claims.subject: Modern stadiums
claims.claim: The biggest stadium in the world is in Seoul and holds 115,000 people.

claims.subject: Circus Maximus
claims.claim: The Circus Maximus is pretty much the wide part of the Colosseum extended like 10 times.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I went over to my mom's house today for about an hour.

claims.subject: Colosseum
claims.claim: I showed my mom the video I made of the Colosseum on her computer.

claims.subject: Colosseum
claims.claim: If you go to my Google Earth download page and click on LA, then Isis and Serapis, Region 3, you'll see the Colosseum there.

claims.subject: Colosseum
claims.claim: I played a YouTube video of a guy walking through the Colosseum while showing my model of it on my mom's computer.

claims.subject: Circus Maximus
claims.claim: After I finish the Circus Maximus, I want to build all the other circuses in the city.

claims.subject: Circus Maximus
claims.claim: There are three other circuses in the city, one at the Vatican, one in the southeastern part, and one farther south.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I want to build the city quicker than before by using simple apartment buildings and filling them up quickly.

claims.subject: Circus Maximus
claims.claim: I want to make the city bigger, reaching out to the circus that was farther south.

claims.subject: Circus Maximus
claims.claim: There are ruins of the circus farther south, including mosaic homes and buildings.

claims.subject: Circus Maximus
claims.claim: I want to visualize each apartment in the city without taking too much time.

claims.subject: Circus Maximus
claims.claim: After finishing the Circus Maximus, I have to build more theaters and another Palatine Hill.

claims.subject: Circus Maximus
claims.claim: I might finish the Circus Maximus tomorrow and then build it into the Palatine Hill.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm studying history by reading Wikipedia's decades, and it's an amazing way to learn.
